conditions.evidence_text: The critical condition was found as 33.3 °C for the 100 Å pore column... Column: Kromasil C18, 100 Å pore, 5 µm particle, 150 × 4.6 mm. Column temperature: 33.3 °C. Eluent: CH2Cl2/CH3CN (57/43, v/v) at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, Pohang University of Science and Technology, Pohang 790-784, Korea
conditions.notes: Used for 1D-LCCC separation of star-shaped PS at the critical condition of linear PS.

conditions.evidence_text: The critical condition was found as... 30.4 °C for the 500 Å pore column... Column: Nucleosil C18, 500 Å pore, 7 µm particle, 250 × 4.6 mm i.d. Column temperature: 30.4 °C. Eluent: CH2Cl2/CH3CN (57/43, v/v) at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, Pohang University of Science and Technology, Pohang 790-784, Korea
conditions.notes: Used for 1D-LCCC separation of star-shaped PS at the critical condition of linear PS.

conditions.evidence_text: For LCCC, column: Kromasil C18, 100 Å pore, 5 µm particle, 150 × 4.6 mm i.d.; column temperature: 28.1 °C; eluent: CH2Cl2/CH3CN (57/43, v/v) at a flow rate of 1.9 mL/min.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, Pohang University of Science and Technology, Pohang 790-784, Korea
conditions.notes: Used as the second dimension in RP-TGIC × LCCC 2D-LC separation. Temperature differs from 1D-LCCC (33.3 °C) likely due to flow rate differences or specific 2D-LC optimization.

conditions.evidence_text: For LCCC column: Nucleosil C18, 500 Å pore, 7 µm particle, 150 × 4.6 mm i.d. Column temperature: 33.5 °C. Eluent: CH2Cl2/CH3CN (57/43, v/v) at a flow rate of 1.9 mL/min.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, Pohang University of Science and Technology, Pohang 790-784, Korea
conditions.notes: Used as the second dimension in RP-TGIC × LCCC 2D-LC separation. Temperature differs from 1D-LCCC (30.4 °C) likely due to flow rate differences or specific 2D-LC optimization.
